description: The figure presents a weak-beam dark-field (WBDF) transmission electron micrograph showing a dissociated dislocation in the base austenitic steel alloy. The main micrograph captures two parallel partial dislocations separated by a stacking fault, with a scale bar of 50 nm. An inset selected-area electron diffraction (SAED) pattern in the upper-right corner confirms the two-beam diffraction condition used for imaging. On-image annotations define the critical measurement parameters: d denotes the partial dislocation separation distance, α represents the angle between the dislocation line vector and the Burgers vector b of the undissociated dislocation, and g//b indicates that the diffraction vector g is parallel to the Burgers vector under the two-beam condition. The sample is the base Fe-Ni-Cr austenitic alloy subjected to 5% true strain compression to introduce dislocations. This image exemplifies the methodology for stacking fault energy (SFE) determination, where paired (d, α) measurements are input into the partial dislocation separation model. The figure supports the finding that the base alloy has an uncorrected SFE of 26.3 ± 3.0 mJ m⁻².
